sections.heading: Data Classification and Information Handling
sections.text_summary: Four-level classification scheme (Confidential, Internal, Public, Restricted), labelling, encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+), physical/electronic media handling, and data retention schedule.

iso.A.5.12: Classification scheme with four tiers defined.
iso.A.5.13: Labelling requirement for all data assets stated.
iso.A.8.24: Encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+) and encryption at rest for Confidential data.

nist.MP-3: Media marking / labelling required for Confidential/Restricted data.
nist.SC-8: Transmission confidentiality — TLS 1.2+ for data in transit.
nist.SC-28: Protection of information at rest — encrypted storage for Confidential data.

sections.heading: Access Control
sections.text_summary: Least privilege, need-to-know, ARF-based provisioning, semi-annual reviews, 4-hour deprovisioning on termination, PAM for privilege, VPN for remote. MFA requirement ABSENT.

iso.A.5.15: Access control policy with least privilege and need-to-know.
iso.A.5.16: Identity management — ARF process, identity management system.
iso.A.8.2: Privileged access rights — PAM system, privileged account registry.
iso.A.8.3: Information access restriction — role profiles, RBAC implied.

nist.AC-1: Access control policy and procedures established.
nist.AC-2: Account management — provisioning, review, deprovisioning lifecycle.
nist.AC-3: Access enforcement — least privilege, role-based provisioning.
nist.AC-17: Remote access — VPN requirement for remote connections.

sections.heading: Incident Response
sections.text_summary: 4-tier incident categorisation (P1-P4), SOC reporting, documented IRP, annual tabletop exercise, post-incident review. Vulnerability scanning ABSENT.

iso.A.5.24: IRP planning and preparation — documented IRP, CISO as owner.
iso.A.5.25: Assessment/decision on incidents — severity categorisation P1-P4.
iso.A.5.26: Response to incidents — response timelines, containment.
iso.A.5.27: Learning from incidents — post-incident review within 5 days.

nist.IR-1: IR policy and procedures — IRP documented.
nist.IR-6: Incident reporting — SOC hotline and portal.
nist.IR-8: Incident response plan — maintained, tested annually.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: No multi-factor authentication (MFA) requirement specified for any access category, including privileged access and remote access.
seeded_gaps.suggested_addition: Section 2 (Access Control) must be amended to require MFA for all remote access sessions, all privileged account logins, and all access to Confidential or Restricted systems.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: No vulnerability scanning or patch management process defined anywhere in the policy. Technical vulnerabilities are not addressed.
seeded_gaps.suggested_addition: A new sub-section must be added to Section 3 (or a standalone Vulnerability Management Policy created) defining: vulnerability scanning frequency, responsible team, patch SLAs by severity (critical=24h, high=7d, medium=30d), and periodic penetration testing schedule.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: Multi-factor authentication not specified for organisational users, particularly for privileged and remote access scenarios.
seeded_gaps.suggested_addition: Access Control section must mandate MFA for privileged accounts, remote access (VPN), and all access to critical/regulated systems.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: Vulnerability monitoring and scanning procedure is absent from the policy. No patch management SLAs defined.
seeded_gaps.suggested_addition: Policy must define a vulnerability management programme including scheduled scanning, CVE tracking, and severity-based remediation timelines.
